description_long: This radio receiver features a rectangular metal body with wooden side panels, giving it a classic, vintage appearance. The front panel is equipped with a blue-lit frequency display, several control knobs, and a large tuning dial, all arranged in a horizontal row. The top surface has ventilation slots for heat dissipation, while the rear panel includes multiple input/output ports and speaker terminals, indicating its functionality for audio connections. The sides are finished in a warm wood tone, contrasting with the metallic silver of the main chassis. The overall design is compact and sturdy, suitable for placement on a shelf or table in an urban environment. The object lacks any visible antenna but is clearly designed for manual operation and audio output.
description: A compact, vintage-style radio receiver with a silver metal body, wooden side panels, a blue-lit frequency display, and multiple control knobs on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue-lit frequency display, control knobs, and large tuning dial, with wooden side panels visible.
description_view_1: Top-down view mainly showing the flat, vented metal top and the overall rectangular shape, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying various input/output ports and speaker terminals, as well as the wooden side panels.
description_view_3: Side view highlighting the wooden panel and the depth of the radio receiver, with the tuning dial slightly visible at the front.
